skill.name: php-pro
skill.description: Guides modern PHP development with generators, iterators, SPL data structures, PHP 8+ features, strict typing, and performance-focused design.
skill.summary: Build idiomatic, type-safe, and memory-efficient PHP applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command finding at SKILL.md:25 is a false positive caused by Markdown backticks around a resource path. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md:3 is also a false positive; the line is descriptive YAML text and contains no cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown File Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:25 uses backticks to format `resources/implementation-playbook.md` as inline Markdown. This is not shell execution and does not run an external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside a documentation instruction and contains no command interpreter, arguments, or execution syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:3 is part of the YAML description for PHP coding guidance. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API usage on this line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains plain descriptive metadata, so the weak cryptography alert has no supporting semantic context.

content.value_statement: PHP teams need code that is fast, readable, and maintainable at production scale. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward idiomatic PHP 8+ patterns, strict typing, and practical performance choices.
actual_capabilities: Recommends generators and iterators for memory-efficient data processing.
Applies PHP 8+ features such as enums, match expressions, attributes, and constructor promotion.
Guides use of SPL data structures when they provide clear performance benefits.
Improves type coverage with union, intersection, never, and mixed types.
Promotes PSR-style organization, namespaces, exceptions, and production-ready error handling.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated PHP tooling.
Does not replace project-specific framework documentation.
Requires the user to provide code, constraints, and runtime context.
Cannot prove performance gains without measurements from the target application.

use_cases.title: Optimize Large Data Processing
use_cases.description: Review a PHP workflow and redesign it with generators, iterators, and streaming patterns to reduce memory usage.

use_cases.title: Modernize Legacy PHP Code
use_cases.description: Refactor older PHP code toward strict types, PHP 8+ syntax, clear exceptions, and safer object design.

use_cases.title: Design Production PHP Components
use_cases.description: Plan classes, namespaces, error handling, and logging hooks for maintainable services and libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Review PHP Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small function or class.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PHP code for readability, type safety, and obvious performance issues. Explain the highest value changes first.

prompt_templates.title: Use Generators
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this data processing flow to use generators or iterators where appropriate. Keep behavior the same and explain tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize for PHP 8
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced refactor of legacy application code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Modernize this PHP code for PHP 8+. Use strict types, enums, match expressions, constructor promotion, and exceptions only where they fit.

prompt_templates.title: Design a PHP Component
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture and production readiness task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production-ready PHP component for this requirement. Include namespaces, interfaces, error handling, logging points, and test scenarios.

output_examples.input: A CSV import runs out of memory on large files.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend streaming file reads, generator-based row handling, bounded batching, validation errors as exceptions, and profiling before deeper optimization.

output_examples.input: A service class uses arrays for every return value.
output_examples.output: The skill would suggest typed value objects or DTOs, explicit return types, clear exception boundaries, and tests for invalid inputs.

output_examples.input: A team wants to update old PHP 7 code.
output_examples.output: The skill would prioritize strict types, constructor property promotion, match expressions, enums for fixed states, and PSR-compliant organization.

best_practices: Provide the PHP version, framework, runtime constraints, and target behavior.
Ask for measured performance guidance before accepting optimization changes.
Request tests for edge cases, invalid inputs, and error paths.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for optimization without profiling data or a clear bottleneck.
Do not use SPL data structures when simple arrays are clearer and fast enough.
Do not add third-party packages before checking PHP standard library options.

faq.question: What PHP versions does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on modern PHP 8+ features while still helping with older code modernization.

faq.question: Can it write framework-specific code?
faq.answer: It can help when you provide framework context, but it is centered on core PHP patterns.

faq.question: Does it execute PHP code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and code recommendations, but it does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Is it useful for performance work?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes profiling, generators, iterators, SPL structures, and memory-aware data processing.

faq.question: Can it improve security?
faq.answer: It can guide safer validation, error handling, and injection prevention patterns in PHP code.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: PHP developers, maintainers, and technical leads who need idiomatic production code.
